title: Inject external regulation and circuit-energy priors as race-level meta-features
summary: The dataset spans 2022-2025, and F1 rules/track characteristics are not fully encoded by `Race` and `Compound` alone.
rationale: The dataset spans 2022-2025, and F1 rules/track characteristics are not fully encoded by `Race` and `Compound` alone. FIA regulations impose dry-race compound-use constraints, and Monaco has a special 2025 three-set race rule. Official F1/Pirelli material also stresses that track energy and degradation regime vary strongly by circuit and drive strategy choice.
implementation_hint: Add race-level features: mandatory dry-compound-change flag, Monaco-2025 special-rule flag, estimated circuit energy/degradation class, expected one-stop vs two-stop tendency, and wet-compound exemption flag. Encode these as simple lookup priors merged by `(Race, Year)` and interact them with tyre-life, stint, and laps remaining.
expected_effect: Captures systematic between-race strategy differences that current frequency encodings likely blur, especially for outlier venues and 2025 rule changes.
risk: Manual priors can be noisy or incomplete; gains depend on correct race-name mapping.
